content.text: HOW TO PERFORM PRAYER
Below is a step-by-step guide on how to perform prayer, using Fajr prayer as an example.
1. Niyyah (Intention)
Intention is made before starting the prayer. Intention is made in the heart - saying it with the tongue is Sunnah. Example: "I intend to perform two rakaats of Fajr Fard for the sake of Allah."
2. Takbir Tahrimah
Both hands are raised to the earlobes, fingers open. "Allahu Akbar" is said as hands are raised, then lowered.
3. Placing of Hands
Men place the right hand over the left hand and hold them below the navel. Women hold them over the chest. "Subhanaka" Dua is recited.
"Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika wa tabarakasmuka wa ta'ala jadduka wa la ilaha ghayruk"
4. Qira'at (Recitation)
"A'udhu" and "Basmalah" are said, then Surah Al-Fatihah and a second Surah (Zam Surah) are recited.

Al-Fatihah: "Bismillahir-rahmanir-rahim. Alhamdu lillahi Rabbil-alamin. Errahmanir-rahim. Maliki yawmiddin. Iyyaka na'budu wa iyyaka nasta'in. Ihdinassiratal-mustaqim. Siratal-ladhina an'amta alayhim. Ghayril-maghdubi alayhim walad-dallin. Amin"
5-6. Performing Ruku' and Rising

items: Perform Ruku' with "Allahu Akbar" takbir, bowing down.
Hold both knees with both hands, fingers open.

content.text: 7-8. Sajdah and Sitting

items: Sajdah is performed with 7 parts: forehead, nose, two hands, two knees, and toes.
Sit up (jalsa), then perform second Sajdah - again say "Subhana Rabbiyal-A'la" three times.

content.text: 9-10. Tashahhud and Sitting
After two rakaats, sitting is performed. "At-Tahiyyat" Dua is recited:

"At-tahiyyatu lillahi was-salawatu wat-tayyibatu. Assalamu alayka ayyuhan-nabiyyu wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh. Assalamu alayna wa ala ibadillahis-salihin. Ashhadu alla ilaha illallah. Wa ashhadu anna Muhammadan abduhu wa Rasuluh"
In the final sitting, "Salat Ibrahim" and Dua are recited.

"Allahumma salli ala Muhammad, wa ala ali Muhammad. Kama sallayta ala Ibrahim, wa ala ali Ibrahim. Innaka hamidun majid. Allahumma barik ala Muhammad, wa ala ali Muhammad. Kama barakta ala Ibrahim, wa ala ali Ibrahim. Innaka hamidun majid"
11. Taslim (Salam)
Turn face to the right and left and give Salam:
"Assalamu alaykum wa rahmatullah"
